title: Select connection node for Actived/Current connection Tab.
author: savaladaojr
body_snippet:
## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
<!--- If you're suggesting a change/improvement, tell us how it should work -->
When the user clicked on a connection node (connections) that is already connected, the nRemoteNG could show up the connection Tab. I mean, could activate the "Tab" related to that connection.

And also, the opposite should be expected behavior. For example:  when the user clicks (not double-click) on the connection TAB, the ap...

iterations.description: When switching between connection tabs, the connection tree should auto-select the corresponding node for better navigation UX.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (claude): When switching between connection tabs, the connection tree should auto-select the corresponding node for better navigation UX.
Approach: Subscribe to the active tab changed event in ConnectionTreeWindow and call tree.SelectNode() with the matching ConnectionInfo node when the active connection changes.
